CrashLabel: qBittorrent-only recurrence after user started qBittorrent

UnexpectedShutdownEvent.Message: The previous system shutdown at 1:11:14 PM on ‎5/‎29/‎2026 was unexpected.

KernelPowerEvent.Message: The system has rebooted without cleanly shutting down first. This error could be caused if the system stopped responding, crashed, or lost power unexpectedly.

WHEAEvent.Message: A fatal hardware error has occurred. A record describing the condition is contained in the data section of this event.

KeyFindings: qBittorrent start and unexpected shutdown line up at roughly 2026-05-29 13:11 local time.
Windows again recorded Kernel-Power 41 with BugcheckCode 0 and a fatal WHEA hardware error after reboot, matching the previous platform-reset pattern.
qBittorrent container was bind-mounted to C:/media-stack/config/qbittorrent and I:/torrentfiles, exposing 6881 TCP/UDP and 8080 TCP.
qBittorrent restores 39 torrent/session files at startup, so startup is immediate disk plus peer/network activity, not idle.
No checked pre-crash disk/storahci/NTFS warning was found; storage volumes were visible after reboot.
Crash-window thermal log initialized but captured no completed samples before the reset; post-reboot sensors show normal temperatures and visible +12 V/+3.3 V readings.
